title: leucanthemum paludosum subsp. decipiens
intro: Leucanthemum paludosum subsp. decipiens is a lesser-known species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It is primarily found in wetlands and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including reducing pain and swelling, and has been used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions. Leucanthemum paludosum subsp. decipiens has been traditionally prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties, although its use and efficacy may vary depending on the region and cultural practices.
